title: The Impact of the Pandemic on Gender Inequality in the Australian Labor Market
abstract: We examine whether the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) and the associated policy responses have aggravated gender inequality in the Australian labor market. Using quarterly data from the Australian Labour Force Survey between November 2019 and November 2021, we compare labor force outcomes before and during the outbreak. Our findings indicate that while women fared worse than men in the first few months of the pandemic, labor market recovery was much more rapid for women. By the end of the period, on most indicators, women’s position in the labor market had improved relative to that of men.
authors_text: Mooi-Reci, Irma; Trinh, Trong-Anh; Wooden, Mark
